reviews.author: Maria L.
reviews.title: Beautiful floral cup
reviews.body: The jasmine notes the description promised really do come through on a careful V60. Smooth body, clean finish, lovely as a black pour-over. Roast date was three days before it landed on my doorstep. Shipping was fast.

reviews.author: Devon P.
reviews.title: Light and tea-like
reviews.body: Brews much more like a delicate tea than what I usually expect from coffee. Took a couple of tries to dial in. Wish it came pre-ground for V60 - my hand grinder isn't fine enough. Once I borrowed my friend's electric grinder it was great.

reviews.author: Sam R.
reviews.title: My favorite Ethiopian
reviews.body: I drink Ethiopian coffees every week and this Yirgacheffe is one of the better ones I've had this year. The bergamot finish is lovely. The bag stayed fresh for a full three weeks because I stored it properly. Highly recommend for pour-over fans.

reviews.author: Jordan H.
reviews.title: Worth the price
reviews.body: Yes the price is up there for 250g but you get what you pay for. The traceability sheet is a nice touch. I've ordered three bags now and the cup quality has been consistent across them. Subscription saved me from having to remember to reorder.

reviews.author: Priya N.
reviews.title: Bright and bold
reviews.body: Blackcurrant, syrupy body, finishes long. Excellent as pour-over and surprisingly nice as espresso for a Kenyan. Ships fast, packaging is sturdy, and the roast date was only two days before I received it. This is my new house coffee for the season.

reviews.author: Marcus B.
reviews.title: Cuts through milk
reviews.body: I drink most of my coffee as flat whites and this Kenyan is the rare bean that survives the milk and keeps its character. Wine-like is the right word. Shipping was fast and the bag arrived in great shape. I'll be reordering.

reviews.author: Theo C.
reviews.title: Aggressive - in a good way
reviews.body: If you don't like bright coffees this isn't the bag for you. I love bright coffees, so I love this. My only complaint is that it took me a few brews to dial in the right grind for it. Some grind size guidance on the bag would help.

reviews.author: Ana K.
reviews.title: Easy daily driver
reviews.body: The Colombia Huila is exactly what the description says - forgiving and balanced. Smooth body, mellow brown sugar finish. I use it for both my Aeropress and my drip machine and it works beautifully in both. Subscription saved me from running out.

reviews.author: Liam D.
reviews.title: Comfort in a cup
reviews.body: This is the bag that lives on my counter year-round. Reliable, sweet, never offends. My partner drinks it with a splash of milk and I take it black. Shipping was fast and the price for 1kg is genuinely reasonable for specialty coffee at this quality.

reviews.author: Sofia E.
reviews.title: Smooth body
reviews.body: Caramel, soft acidity, smooth body, easy mornings. I gave a bag to my coffee-skeptic father in law and he texted me asking what brand it was. That's a five star outcome. Will keep ordering. The freshness on arrival was noticeable.

reviews.author: Henry G.
reviews.title: Solid every day cup
reviews.body: Honestly nothing remarkable but in the best way - a coffee you can rely on every morning. The Colombian profile, well executed. Not as bright as the Kenya or as wild as the Yemen, just a good consistent cup. Roast date was fresh on arrival.

reviews.author: Isabella V.
reviews.title: Cocoa-forward and dense
reviews.body: The smoky, dense body the description mentions is real. I pulled it as a single origin espresso for a week straight and it was excellent. Toasted hazelnut for days. Shipping was fast and the bag was fresh. The Guatemala is now in my regular rotation.

reviews.author: Carlos R.
reviews.title: Volcanic in the best way
reviews.body: I've drunk a lot of Antigua coffees over the years and this one is in my top five. Full body, soft acidity, brown sugar. Brilliant in milk drinks. The traceability info on the bag is a thoughtful touch. Reordering already. Shipping was fast.

reviews.author: Nadia W.
reviews.title: Solid Central American
reviews.body: Good cup, balanced, slightly heavier than I usually drink but worth a try if you're tired of bright African coffees. Brewed it as French press at first and that was almost too much body for me. AeroPress was the sweet spot. Bag arrived very fresh.

reviews.author: Ahmed Z.
reviews.title: The chocolate bomb
reviews.body: Brazil Cerrado from Fulcrum has been my espresso anchor for six months. Roasted peanut, milk chocolate, exactly what the description says. Pulls a beautiful shot, disappears into a flat white. Subscription saved me - I'd run out constantly otherwise.

reviews.author: Ellen M.
reviews.title: Perfect espresso bean
reviews.body: I bought this for my home espresso setup and it has not disappointed. Pulls 28-30 second shots consistently with the recipe Fulcrum publishes on the bag. Marshmallow body lives up to the hype. Smooth body in milk drinks. Shipping was fast.

reviews.author: Felix O.
reviews.title: Forgiving and great
reviews.body: I'm not a precision brewer. I scoop, eyeball, and drink. The Brazil Cerrado forgives my approximate methods better than any other coffee I've bought from Fulcrum. Always tastes good even when I'm half asleep. The 1kg bag is a great value.

reviews.author: Grace L.
reviews.title: Daily driver
reviews.body: This is the bag we go through fastest in our house. Two adults, daily espresso plus pour-over, kills a 500g bag in eight days. Subscription is the only way we keep up. Always fresh, always good. Smooth body and the chocolate-and-nuts cup the description promises.

reviews.author: Rohan T.
reviews.title: Earthy and unmistakable
reviews.body: Sumatra is my desert island coffee and Fulcrum's Mandheling is one of the best examples I've had at this price. Cedar, dark chocolate, low acid. Brewed it in French press and it was outstanding. Shipping was fast and the bag arrived very fresh.

reviews.author: Beatrice F.
reviews.title: Best French press bean
reviews.body: I have been drinking French press almost exclusively for ten years and this Mandheling is the platonic French press bean. Heavy body, no sourness, herbal-cedar finish. Smooth body throughout. The 500g bag lasts me 12 days. Will reorder.

reviews.author: Quentin S.
reviews.title: Strong and earthy
reviews.body: If you like fruity and bright this is not for you. If you like big bold low-acid coffee this is exactly what you want. I take it as moka pot in the morning. Sometimes I find it a bit too earthy when brewed weak. Bag arrived fresh and in good shape.

reviews.author: Yumi A.
reviews.title: Honey-process magic
reviews.body: The honey-process Tarrazu is so different from washed Costa Ricans I've had before. Honeyed apricot, citrus zest, clean finish. Smooth body that's heavier than washed but lighter than naturals. Perfect Kalita Wave brew. Subscription saved me from running out.

reviews.author: Daniel K.
reviews.title: Surprised by the body
reviews.body: I was expecting a brighter, lighter cup based on the Costa Rican coffees I've had before. The honey process gives this real body without losing the citrus. Brewed as V60 and it was excellent. Shipping was fast and the roast date was very recent.

reviews.author: Olivia P.
reviews.title: Like a dessert wine
reviews.body: The Rwanda Nyamasheke is the most surprising bag I've bought from Fulcrum. Red grape, kiwi, black tea body - it really does taste like a dessert wine. Treated it like a special occasion bag. Brewed it patiently and it rewarded me. Shipping was fast.

reviews.author: Patrick J.
reviews.title: Something different
reviews.body: I rotate through African coffees and the Rwanda was the standout this season. More delicate than Kenya, less floral than Ethiopia, juicy in a way I haven't tasted from this continent before. Highly recommend if you're tired of the usual suspects. Bag was fresh.

reviews.author: Kira E.
reviews.title: Lovely but pricey
reviews.body: The cup is genuinely beautiful - Fulcrum nails the description. My only hesitation is the price for 250g. I understand it's a small lot from a remote washing station but it does limit how often I can buy it. Subscription saved me on the rest of my orders though.

reviews.author: Vincent O.
reviews.title: Quietly excellent
reviews.body: Honduras Marcala is the bag I keep recommending to friends who think they don't like specialty coffee. Caramel, walnut, soft cocoa - comfort food in a cup. Smooth body, super forgiving. Shipping was fast and the bag stayed fresh through 12 days of brewing.

reviews.author: Helen W.
reviews.title: Underrated origin
reviews.body: I had never bought a Honduran coffee before and now I'm a convert. The Marcala is balanced, sweet, and incredibly approachable. My drip machine treats it well, my Aeropress treats it better. Smooth body, gentle finish. Subscription is on for this one now.

reviews.author: Tobias R.
reviews.title: Clean and gentle
reviews.body: Peru Cajamarca is the cleanest washed South American I've had this year. Cane sugar, green apple, light floral finish. Tea-like body. Brewed exclusively as pour-over. The organic-by-default story is a nice bonus. Shipping was fast and the bag was very fresh.

reviews.author: Maya G.
reviews.title: Soft and contemplative
reviews.body: This is a coffee for slow mornings. Not the boldest in Fulcrum's lineup but really lovely if you're paying attention. I had to grind it slightly finer than usual to get full extraction in my V60. Bag was fresh and shipping was fast. Will reorder when in stock.

reviews.author: Sebastian L.
reviews.title: Mexican chocolate in a cup
reviews.body: The cinnamon-edged cocoa profile is real. This is the most distinctively Mexican Mexican coffee I've had - usually they all just taste generic. The Chiapas has a clear identity. Smooth body, soft acidity. Pulls a forgiving espresso shot too. Shipping was fast.

reviews.author: Adriana C.
reviews.title: Hidden gem
reviews.body: I bought this on a whim and it has become my second favorite bag in the catalog. Quieter than the Brazil but more interesting. Cocoa, cinnamon, raw almond. The price is great for the quality. Subscription saved me from forgetting to reorder.

reviews.author: Reza F.
reviews.title: Time travel in a cup
reviews.body: The Yemen Mocha Sanani is unlike any other coffee I've ever tasted. Dried fig, raisin, cardamom - it tastes ancient, in the best way. The price reflects the rarity and I think it's fair. Treated each cup as a sipping experience. Shipping was fast and packaging was excellent.

reviews.author: Lily H.
reviews.title: Worth the splurge
reviews.body: I drank this slowly over two weeks. Wild, syrupy, winey, with notes I genuinely could not have predicted. Brewed it as a slow pour-over and as French press; both worked. This is the bag I send people who say they've tasted everything. Shipping was fast.

reviews.author: Greg J.
reviews.title: Polarizing
reviews.body: Look, this is a unique coffee, but it's also $38 for 250g and the natural-process funkiness is really intense. I think it's a love or hate kind of bag. I respect it more than I enjoyed it. Shipping was fast at least. Maybe save it for a special occasion brew.

reviews.author: Natalie B.
reviews.title: Perfect every morning
reviews.body: We go through a 1kg bag of Morning Blend every two weeks. The balance between body and brightness is exactly right for a daily cup. My partner drinks it with milk, I drink it black, both work. Subscription saved me from constantly reordering. Smooth body throughout.

reviews.author: Connor M.
reviews.title: House coffee for two years
reviews.body: This has been our house coffee since I discovered Fulcrum two years ago. The component ratios change slightly each season but the cup signature stays the same. Always fresh, always good. The 1kg bag at this price is one of the best deals in specialty coffee.

reviews.author: Sienna D.
reviews.title: Forgiving and balanced
reviews.body: I'm a beginner home brewer and the Morning Blend is the most forgiving bag in the lineup. Even when my pour technique falls apart, the cup is still good. The Yirgacheffe lift on the finish is a nice touch. Shipping was fast and the bag arrived very fresh.

reviews.author: Ravi P.
reviews.title: Solid blend
reviews.body: Good blend, well executed. I drink mostly single origins so a blend has to really earn its place in my rotation. The Morning Blend is good enough that I keep it around for when guests want coffee and they're not specialty drinkers. Smooth body, easy.

reviews.author: Aaron T.
reviews.title: Exceptional shots
reviews.body: I've been pulling shots at home for eight years and Fulcrum's Espresso Blend is in my top three. Body, crema, chocolate-and-cocoa profile, holds up under milk. The recipe printed on the bag is a great starting point - I dialed in within three shots. Shipping was fast.

reviews.author: Mira A.
reviews.title: Sweet under milk
reviews.body: My Linea Mini is happy with this blend. The 1kg bag at this price is great value for daily espresso. Cup is sweet under milk for flat whites and balanced as a black shot. Subscription saved me from running out mid week. Smooth body always.

reviews.author: Owen H.
reviews.title: Bag has the recipe
reviews.body: I love that they print the dial in recipe on the bag. Saved me a lot of trial and error. The shot pulls as advertised. Body is heavy without being muddy. Decent as French press too when my espresso machine was being descaled. Bag arrived very fresh.

reviews.author: Cynthia R.
reviews.title: Heavier than I expected
reviews.body: Good shot, lots of body, maybe slightly too dark for my taste. I prefer a brighter espresso. That said the consistency across bags is excellent and my husband loves it for cappuccinos. Shipping was fast. Wish there was a lighter espresso option in the lineup.

reviews.author: Frances K.
reviews.title: Decent decaf
reviews.body: Decent decaf. Swiss water process so no chemical taste. The cup is on the simpler side compared to their full caf coffees but that's the trade off with decaffeination. Shipping was fast. I wish the description told me a bit more about what to expect.

reviews.author: Bryan L.
reviews.title: Fine but generic
reviews.body: Honestly the bag and description don't say much and the cup matches - fine but generic. I get that decaf is harder to make exciting but I expected more from Fulcrum given how detailed their other product pages are. Shipping was fast at least.

reviews.author: Tessa M.
reviews.title: Great for cold brew
reviews.body: Smooth body, sweet, low bitterness. Cold brewed it as concentrate at 1:8 for 16 hours and it was excellent diluted with milk. The 1kg bag lasts me about three batches. Shipping was fast. I do wish the bag suggested which single origins also work for cold brew.

reviews.author: Julian S.
reviews.title: Cold brew specialist
reviews.body: Does what it says on the bag. Brewed great cold brew with the recipe Fulcrum suggests. Sweet, chocolatey, smooth body. Not super exciting hot - wouldn't be my pour over pick. But for batch cold brew it's exactly the bag I needed. Bag was fresh on arrival.

reviews.author: Carmen E.
reviews.title: Ceramic makes a difference
reviews.body: Switched from a plastic V60 to this ceramic one and the cup is noticeably sweeter, even with the same beans and recipe. Pre-warming it is essential. Came well packaged, arrived intact. Shipping was fast. Worth the upgrade if you've been using plastic.

reviews.author: Wesley A.
reviews.title: Beautiful clean cup
reviews.body: I bought the Chemex specifically for our African washed coffees and it does exactly what it should - produces a clean, almost wine-like cup that lets origin character through. Pre-rinse the filter. Shipping was fast and the glass arrived intact thanks to good packaging.

reviews.author: Ingrid V.
reviews.title: The best beginner brewer
reviews.body: Bought this for my partner who was new to home brewing. They were making great coffee within a week. So forgiving across grind sizes and recipes. Smooth body, super versatile. Shipping was fast and the box was complete with all the parts.

reviews.author: Marlon C.
reviews.title: Classic for a reason
reviews.body: I've owned three Bodum Chambords over the years. They eventually break (it's glass) but the design is the design. This 1L is the right size for a couple. Use coarse grind, four minutes, slow press. Heavier body than any paper filter brew. Shipping was fast.

reviews.author: Saoirse F.
reviews.title: Game changer
reviews.body: Single biggest upgrade to my home brewing. The Acaia Pearl makes my pour overs consistent in a way nothing else has. The app integration is a nice bonus. Yes the price is high. Yes it is worth it. Shipping was fast and the device arrived in perfect condition.

reviews.author: Hugo P.
reviews.title: The pour control is real
reviews.body: I had a cheap gooseneck before this. The Stagg EKG is in another category. The pour rate is genuinely controllable, the temperature precision actually matters, and the matte black finish is gorgeous. Worth the upgrade. Shipping was fast and packaging was excellent.

reviews.author: Eleanor B.
reviews.title: Subscription saved me
reviews.body: Subscription saved me from constantly running out and from getting stuck in a single bean rut. New origin every week, brewing notes included, and the bags are always fresh. Best value for an exploratory specialty coffee drinker. The skip-week option is essential when traveling.

reviews.author: Jasper M.
reviews.title: Monthly is the right cadence
reviews.body: Weekly was too much for our two-person household. Monthly 500g is the sweet spot. We finish each bag right around when the next ships. Origin rotation keeps things interesting. Subscription saved me from forgetting to reorder. Shipping was fast every time.

reviews.author: Tahlia R.
reviews.title: The most considered subscription
reviews.body: The quarterly sampler is the most considered coffee subscription I've had. Four 100g bags, deliberate theme, great booklet. The pace is slow enough that I actually finish each bag and form an opinion. Highly recommended for anyone who wants to learn while drinking.

reviews.author: Phoebe O.
reviews.title: Best onboarding gift
reviews.body: Bought this for a friend who just got a V60. They texted me three days in saying they finally understood why I'm so into coffee. The four origin map across continents is a great teaching set. Shipping was fast. The booklet inside is genuinely useful.

reviews.author: Victor H.
reviews.title: Lovely shape
reviews.body: The 12oz size is great - the coffee actually stays warm long enough to drink it. Hand-thrown shape feels nicer than a typical printed mug. The matte cream Fulcrum mark on slate gray is understated and good looking. Shipping was fast and the mug arrived intact.

reviews.author: Rosa L.
reviews.title: The book seals it
reviews.body: Gifted this to my sister who just got into specialty coffee. The Hoffmann book is the perfect primer and the Colombia Huila is forgiving enough that her early brews were drinkable. Smooth body. The combination is genuinely thoughtful. Shipping was fast.

reviews.author: Elliot G.
reviews.title: Complete starter set
reviews.body: Bought for my dad's birthday. He was brewing solid V60s within his first week. The kit having everything in one shipment is a major part of the value - he didn't have to research three separate purchases. Shipping was fast and the packaging was excellent.

reviews.author: Chiamaka N.
reviews.title: Strawberry jam in liquid form
reviews.body: The Guji microlot is the best Ethiopian I've had this year, and that includes a few well known competitor lots. Strawberry, peach, syrupy body - extraordinary. Glad I bought two bags before they sold out. Subscription saved me on regulars; this was a planned splurge.

reviews.author: Anand V.
reviews.title: Colombian Geisha is real
reviews.body: I had only ever had Panamanian Geisha before. The Colombian version from this microlot is genuinely on par at a fraction of the price. Jasmine, bergamot, peach, electric. Brewed it three different ratios over the bag. The 200g vanished quickly. Shipping was fast.
